title: Cost spike attribution → fallback to current-cost view (stable demo env)
description: User notices a perceived bill jump and asks for attribution + safe-mute candidates. Canonical chain on a GROWING env: cost_drivers → dependency_check → exclusion_filter. On the OTel demo env (which replays continuously and is stable by construction), cost_drivers correctly returns 'no cost drivers detected' and chains into the fallback path (top_patterns / savings / investigate). Either chain is acceptable; the test enforces the LCS subsequence + that cost_drivers ran first and a safety/triage tool ran somewhere downstream.
prompt: Our log bill jumped this week. What's driving it and what's safest to mute?

initial_tool.reason: Bill-jumped questions are CHANGE-over-time questions. cost_drivers is the dollar-ranked delta tool; top_patterns would mislead with current-rank-not-growth.

ground_truth.description: cost_drivers result is well-formed: either reports drivers OR reports a truthful 'no drivers detected' negative result with comparison
ground_truth.pattern: (cost driver|no cost drivers detected|growth deltas|truthful negative result)

ground_truth.description: cost_drivers emits a NEXT_ACTIONS hint (either dependency_check on the success path or top_patterns/savings/investigate on the no-growth fallback path)
the chain invoked a downstream triage tool (top_patterns / dependency_check / investigate) — not just cost_drivers in isolation
